source_jobs.title: Senior Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
HeyMilo is redefining how companies hire with agentic AI—backed by leading Silicon Valley investors and trusted by top staffing agencies, global BPOs, and Fortune 100 enterprises. Our AI agents automate candidate engagement, eliminate fraud, accelerate screening, and enable hiring teams to make dramatically better decisions with unmatched speed and accuracy.



As demand accelerates across every segment of the recruiting industry, we are rapidly expanding our GTM organization. We’re hiring A-player Account Executives who are obsessed with winning, thrive in high-performance environments, and want to play a defining role in shaping a rocket-ship company. This role is for someone who takes pride in excellence, owns their outcomes end-to-end, and wants to close meaningful deals that reshape how the world hires.



What You Will Do

 - Own the full sales cycle—prospecting, discovery, demos, solutioning, negotiation, and closing.

 - Run complex 5- and 6-figure deals with multi-stakeholder buying groups across HR, TA, Operations, IT, Compliance, and Finance.

 - Sell a category-leading product by demonstrating how HeyMilo outperforms competitors in accuracy, speed, fraud detection, and real-world complexity.

 - Lead strategic conversations with executives across staffing agencies, BPOs, and mid-market to Fortune 100 corporates.

 - Diagnose workflows and hiring pain points to drive urgency and ROI-focused decision-making.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with Product, Engineering, and Customer Success to ensure smooth handoffs and long-term customer success.

 - Execute with speed, precision, and ownership to help capture market share in a rapidly growing category.



Who You Are

 - A top-performing AE who consistently exceeds quota and holds themselves to elite standards.

 - A true full-cycle seller capable of independently running outbound, discovery, demo, solution design, negotiation, and close.

 - Experienced navigating complex 5- and 6-figure SaaS deals and presenting to senior leaders.

 - A clear, confident communicator who can simplify technical concepts and speak credibly to executives.

 - Highly competitive with a strong internal drive to win and outperform alternatives.

 - Thrives in a high-growth, fast-moving environment where urgency, ownership, and excellence are the baseline.

 - Have sold agentic AI capabilities to organizations previously

 - Curious, product-minded, and comfortable understanding integrations, data flows, and real-world hiring operations.

 - Customer-obsessed and committed to delivering outcomes—not just closing deals.


Why Join HeyMilo

 - Join a hyper-growth company transforming high-volume hiring across staffing, corporate recruiting, and BPOs.

 - Sell a product that consistently wins competitive evaluations and delivers clear, measurable ROI.

 - Work alongside a team of top performers who expect excellence and celebrate big wins.

 - Own high-impact deals that directly influence HeyMilo’s growth and market position.

 - Be part of a culture rooted in grit, speed, accountability, and results.

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer - Full Stack
source_jobs.description:
Senior Software Engineer - Full Stack // Colombo

HeyMilo helps recruiters deploy multimodal AI agents that interview and evaluate candidates at scale. We're a fast-moving team backed by prominent investors growing at an unprecedented rate.

You'll own the recruiter-facing admin platform and the candidate interview experience end-to-end. This means shipping features, refactoring legacy flows, killing dead code, and making sure every screen a recruiter or candidate touches actually works well -- not just functionally, but experientially. You'll work closely with our UX and backend leads to make sure nothing ships half-baked.

This is not a ticket queue. You'll be embedded in customer conversations, understand what recruiters actually need, and turn that into clean, well-architected product.

Requires flexibility to work evenings (IST) for overlap with our New York/Toronto teams.

What you'll do

 - Own the admin dashboard (agent creation, interview templates, email config, analytics) and the candidate-facing interview app (voice, video, SMS flows)

 - Refactor and consolidate -- we have duplicate components, dead code, and flows that grew organically for individual customers. You'll architect reusable patterns and clean the codebase methodically, feature by feature, not micro-fix by micro-fix

 - Ship full-stack features end-to-end. The backend engineer builds the API, you make the product experience excellent before it reaches a customer

 - Participate in customer calls and read session recordings to understand real user scenarios, not just ticket descriptions

 - Enforce a quality bar on UX before features go live -- if the flow doesn't make sense to a recruiter, it doesn't ship

What we're looking for

 - 2-4 years of professional software engineering experience

 - Strong CS fundamentals -- OOP principles, clean architecture, component design patterns. We will test for this

 - Deep experience with React, Next.js, and TypeScript in production

 - Comfortable with Tailwind CSS, Radix UI / shadcn, and modern component patterns (react-hook-form, Zod, TanStack)

 - Product instinct -- you think about what the user is trying to accomplish, not just whether the function returns the right value

 - You can take a messy, organically-grown feature and refactor it into something scalable without breaking production

 - You use AI tools (Cursor, Devin) to write code daily

 - You ship fast, but you also know when to push back and say "this needs another day to be done right"

 - Bonus: familiarity with WebRTC, voice/video interfaces, or i18n

Role is not for you if

 - You treat frontend as "just CSS" -- this is architecture work

 - You need someone to hand you pixel-perfect designs before you can start

 - You can't explain why your code is structured the way it is

 - You're not comfortable joining a customer call to understand what they actually need

 - You avoid refactoring because "it works"

Why you won't regret it

 - Real-time AI interview platform used by enterprise recruiters daily

 - Small team, high ownership, no bureaucracy

 - Direct line to customers -- you'll see the impact of your work within days

 - Awesome culture with a global team across New York, Toronto, and Colombo

 - Competitive salary with benefits/allowances

source_jobs.title: SEO & Organic Growth Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT HEYMILO



HeyMilo is an AI recruiting platform that helps staffing firms, enterprises, and BPOs automate the repeatable parts of hiring so their recruiters can focus on the work that actually requires a human. We're early-stage, moving fast, and building the category from the ground up.





THE ROLE

We're looking for an SEO & Organic Growth Lead to own our search presence and build the organic acquisition engine.

You'll be the first dedicated organic hire, reporting to the head of marketing, working as a true partner on content strategy, technical decisions, and how we show up across search and AI-generated discovery surfaces. This is an individual contributor role to start, with room to build a function as the company scales.





WHAT YOU'LL OWN



Technical SEO
Site health, crawlability, Core Web Vitals, indexation, schema markup, internal linking, and everything that determines whether we rank at all. You don't need to write production code, but you need to know enough to work directly and influence frontend code and web infra, making smart tradeoffs when speed vs. SEO integrity is on the table.



AEO and AI Search Visibility
This is newer territory for everyone. You'll figure out how HeyMilo should show up in AI-generated answers, LLM-surface recommendations, and emerging discovery contexts. You'll test, learn, and build a point of view, we're not looking for someone who already has it all figured out, because no one has.



Keyword Strategy and Content Roadmap
You'll own the research and prioritization layer: which topics we go after, in what order, at what depth. You'll work closely with marketing on the actual content, writing support is available, but the strategy and briefing logic is yours.



Organic Performance Reporting
Clear visibility into what's working: rankings, organic traffic, lead attribution from organic channels, and what's moving or stalling. We want someone who builds a real measurement practice, not just a dashboard that nobody looks at.



Paid-Organic Feedback Loop
We run Google Ads campaigns and we want to make sure what we learn there (keyword intent, conversion signals, ICP behavior) informs what we build organically and vice versa.





WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

You've done real technical SEO work not just the strategic layer, but the actual auditing, diagnosing, fixing, and iterating. You're comfortable in tools like Screaming Frog, Keyword Heo, Ahrefs, Search Console, and you know how to read a crawl report and turn it into a prioritized action list.

You also think about organic growth more broadly. You understand that a technically clean site that publishes nothing useful goes nowhere. You have opinions about content depth, topical authority, and what actually earns links vs. what just fills a calendar.

You're used to working without much infrastructure around you. There's no team to delegate to, no established playbook to follow, and no slow approval chains. You figure things out, move, and tell people what you learned.

Some familiarity with AI-adjacent content and B2B SaaS contexts is a plus. The HR tech space has its own search dynamics (high-intent, niche audiences, buyers who do real research), and some pattern recognition there goes a long way.

Experience range: We're open on years, but realistically you've been doing this for at least 3-4 years in contexts where you had real ownership.





WHAT THIS IS NOT

This is not a role where you'll hand off a strategy doc and wait for a team to execute it. There's no team yet. If you're energized by that, great. If you need existing infrastructure to be effective, this probably isn't the right fit right now.


HeyMilo is an equal opportunity employer. We hire based on what you can do and how you work, and we're building a team where people with different backgrounds and perspectives make the product and company better.

notes: Multiple identical Account Executive postings, single tech role, and SEO role suggest a sales-heavy org with some tech presence.
